query_text:
Sequential LLM decoding is slow because each token needs a full
large-model forward pass; how can a small draft model propose
tokens in parallel that the large model verifies without quality
loss?
paraphrase_essence: Sequential single-step inference incurs O(N) expensive forward passes when N items must be decided in order, even when many decisions could be made in parallel.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive generation is slow because each decoding step requires a full forward pass over the current context length with an updated KV cache across all attention heads; latency scales linearly with token count even when many continuations are locally predictable.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language models, inference is slow because every generated token triggers cross-modal alignment between image embeddings and visual tokens via query-conditioned attention; end-to-end latency grows with caption length while VQA accuracy hinges on repeatedly re-scoring largely stable visual evidence.
paraphrases.CV: In dense perception, processing is slow when each patch token on a high-resolution feature map must be exhaustively scored with a large receptive field; segmentation quality depends on this per-location computation even though most regions are background in the segmentation mask.
paraphrases.RL: Model-based control is slow because the policy must evaluate long-horizon rollouts and propagate a value function over trajectories before informative reward signals accrue; decision latency scales with horizon even when many action branches are clearly low-value.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, cross-encoder reranking is costly because each (query, document) pair in a large candidate pool requires an expensive query–document score; recall@k depends on this step even though most candidates are clearly irrelevant.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Streaming ASR is slow because decoding proceeds one unit at a time and each acoustic frame requires attention over the full spectrogram under tight alignment constraints; streaming latency scales with utterance length and WER worsens if this computation is reduced.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In closed-loop manipulation, the sensorimotor loop is slow when the manipulation policy recomputes a motion plan at each control cycle by querying a dynamics model with detailed contact reasoning; control latency grows with horizon and task performance degrades if this per-step computation is curtailed.

query_text:
VLM inference latency scales with visual token count but most
tokens are redundant; how can we identify and remove or merge
low-information visual tokens at inference without retraining
the encoder?
paraphrase_essence: Per-input inference cost scales with the number of tokens even though many are redundant, and with frozen encoders there is no inference-time mechanism to compress uninformative tokens without degrading output quality.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive inference is slow when context length is large because each decoding step updates the KV cache and runs every attention head over all tokens, yet many tokens contribute little information and cannot be collapsed under a fixed tokenizer and frozen weights.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: VLM forward latency scales with visual token count in the image embedding; many patch embeddings are redundant background, but with a frozen vision encoder and fixed cross-modal alignment there is no inference-time mechanism to reduce token multiplicity without risking VQA accuracy and caption fidelity.
paraphrases.CV: Dense vision inference is costly at high spatial resolution because per-patch token processing grows with feature map size; large low-saliency regions dominate compute, and with a fixed backbone and receptive field tiling there is no way to consolidate redundant areas without degrading detection mAP and segmentation masks.
paraphrases.RL: Policy evaluation is compute-bound when trajectory length and observation dimensionality are high, as each rollout processes full observations and updates the value function; many components carry little reward signal across steps, yet with a frozen policy there is no online mechanism to compress them without altering behavior.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Serving latency grows with the candidate pool because cross-encoder reranking requires a query-document score for each pair; many candidates are near-duplicates or low-utility, and with a fixed reranker and embedding index there is no way to collapse redundancy without affecting recall@k.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Streaming ASR latency scales with acoustic frame count since decoding consumes every spectrogram frame; long stretches of silence or steady-state audio are redundant, yet with fixed phoneme alignment constraints and an unchanged acoustic model there is no inference-time mechanism to merge frames without hurting WER.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Closed-loop control is inefficient at high-rate operation because the sensorimotor loop processes every cycle over long horizons; successive observations during quasi-static motion are redundant, yet with a fixed manipulation policy, dynamics model, and motion plan there is no online mechanism to compress steps without compromising contact reasoning.
